title: manihot caerulescens var. genuina
intro: Manihot caerulescens var. genuina, also known as the Wild Cassava or Blue Manioc, is a tropical plant native to Central and South America. It is a shrubby perennial plant belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family and is known for its edible and medicinal properties. The plant's roots and leaves have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Manihot caerulescens var. genuina can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or topical ointment to utilize its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
